description: In Turbofan's inlining phase, JSCallReducer::ReduceCallOrConstructWithArrayLikeOrSpread() reads the array length from an AllocationSite boilerplate cached in the FeedbackVector for spread-call nodes (e.g., fn(...arr)). If the boilerplate JSArray length has been corrupted to a negative value via sandbox memory corruption, new_argument_count becomes negative and the loop that inserts input nodes for each array element is skipped entirely. The negative arity is then encoded into a 27-bit ArityField in CallParameters, truncating it to a large positive value. NodeProperties::ReplaceEffectInput() subsequently calls node->ReplaceInput() using this truncated arity as an index offset (via FirstEffectIndex -> FirstEffectIndex + corrupted ValueInputCount), far beyond the node's actual allocated inputs. This out-of-bounds ReplaceInput() reads stale Node* and Use* pointers from adjacent heap memory and manipulates the Use linked list through Node::RemoveUse(), resulting in an arbitrary address write primitive that can be used to escape the V8 sandbox.

fixes.evidence_text: [turbofan] Fix Call argument count mismatch for array literals
fixes.description: Local patch exported from https://crrev.com/c/6478570 for reproducible fixed-image builds.
